_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of distributed data processing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Flink, Beam) and when each is appropriate for a given data volume, latency, and complexity requirement.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-K-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-K-004 (AI, cosine: 0.488), AI-K-015 (AI, cosine: 0.487).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.6/10 (raw 0.264). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [kafka, providers, stream] vs WIDAI-only terms [appropriate, beam, complexity, data, distributed, flink] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.887 (P=0.911, R=0.865). P exceeds R by 0.046 — the DCWF element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 24 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:0.0%, N:57.0%, C:43.0%). No dominant classification for "Knowledge of stream providers (e.g. KAFKA)." vs "Knowledge of distributed data processing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Flink, Beam) a" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.488 > STS 0.264 (gap +0.224). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.4%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [kafka, providers, stream]. WIDAI-only: [appropriate, beam, complexity, data, distributed, flink].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.224 with low STS 0.264. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
